issues: Official content is proof by mathematical induction, but the canonical catalog has no induction node.
The mapped proof node REAS-PROF-PROF-DEDU is proof by deduction, not induction.
The divisibility context is unmapped, and ALGF-MATR-CAYL-POWR concerns Cayley-Hamilton powers rather than inductive matrix powers.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add canonical nodes for mathematical induction and divisibility proofs; until then treat the induction method as unmapped.

issues: ALGF-CPLX-POLAR-MODA is not central to this point; the core content is Euler/exponential form.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain mapping; consider whether the modulus-argument node is needed here.

issues: Solving three linear simultaneous equations using the inverse matrix is not represented by a dedicated canonical node; only the inverse-matrix operation is mapped.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a canonical node for solving linear systems with matrices.

issues: Geometric interpretation of three planes (meeting in a point, sheaf, prism/inconsistent) is not represented in the canonical catalog; only singular/non-singular matrices are mapped.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a canonical node for geometric configurations of three planes.
No change required; the scalar-product node legitimately covers the perpendicular-check application.
No change required; logarithmic forms are an application within inverse hyperbolic functions.
No change required; the auxiliary-equation node covers the discriminant-solution link.

issues: Canonical node TRIG-IDEN-IDEN-TANR represents the basic tan theta identity, not the t-formulae (half-angle substitution) in FP1 1.1. The semantic fit is weak even though the relation is marked partial.
pointReviews.recommendation: If a dedicated t-formulae node is added to the catalog, remap to it; otherwise leave this point unmapped rather than forcing a match to tan identity.
No change required; the generic identities node acceptably covers t-formula identity derivations.
No change required; the generic trigonometric equations node acceptably covers t-formula equation solving.

issues: Official text supports L'Hospital's rule on p.24 2.4.
No canonical node covers L'Hospital's rule; candidate correctly left the point unmapped.
Candidate relation is 'partial' but should be 'unmapped' when no nodes are assigned.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a canonical node for L'Hospital's rule or keep the point unmapped; set relation to unmapped.

issues: Official text supports tangents and normals to conics on p.25 4.3.
Mapping is only to the parent GEOM-CONI-CONI node; the catalog has no dedicated node for tangents/normals to conic sections.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a dedicated conic-section tangents/normals node to the catalog, or accept the weak parent-node mapping.

issues: Official text supports conic loci problems on p.25 4.4.
Mapping is only to the parent GEOM-CONI-CONI node; the catalog has no dedicated node for conic loci.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a dedicated conic loci node to the catalog, or accept the weak parent-node mapping.

issues: Official text supports group axioms on p.27 1.1.
The supplied catalog contains no group-theory nodes; candidate correctly left the point unmapped.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add group-theory nodes to the catalog or retain unmapped status.

issues: Official text supports examples of groups, Cayley tables and cyclic groups on p.27 1.2.
The supplied catalog contains no group-theory nodes; candidate correctly left the point unmapped.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add group-theory nodes to the catalog or retain unmapped status.

pointReviews.recommendation: Add group-theory nodes to the catalog or retain unmapped status.

pointReviews.recommendation: Add group-theory nodes to the catalog or retain unmapped status.

issues: Official text supports isomorphism of groups on p.27 1.5.
The supplied catalog contains no group-theory nodes; candidate correctly left the point unmapped.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add group-theory nodes to the catalog or retain unmapped status.

issues: Official text supports combinatorics on p.29 §5.7.
SyllabusPointId prefix 'CP' is misleading: section 5.7 belongs to Paper 4A Further Pure Mathematics 2 (Number theory), not Core Pure.

Mapped nodes PROB-COMB-COUNT-PERM and PROB-COMB-COUNT-COMB cover permutations and combinations but do not cover the multiplicative/addition/subtraction counting principles or set-notation requirements.
paperApplicability is 'not-specified', so no unsupported paper claim is made.
pointReviews.recommendation: Correct the syllabusPointId prefix to FP2; broaden the mapping or add counting-principles nodes to the catalog.

Canonical mapping to Poisson hypothesis test is exact, but paperApplicability lists 9FM0/04B Further Statistics 2 as eligible, which is not supported by the official specification for Further Statistics 1 content.
pointReviews.recommendation: Remove 9FM0/04B Further Statistics 2 from the eligible papers; retain only the Further Statistics 1 paper mapping.

issues: No exact canonical node exists for a geometric-parameter hypothesis test, so mapping to the general hypothesis-testing node is acceptable but broad.
Paper applicability incorrectly includes 9FM0/04B Further Statistics 2.
pointReviews.recommendation: List 9FM0/04B Further Statistics 2 as ineligible; create or request a more specific node for geometric-distribution hypothesis tests if available.

issues: Exact canonical fit for the Central Limit Theorem, but the listed eligible paper 9FM0/04B Further Statistics 2 is not supported.
pointReviews.recommendation: Restrict paper applicability to Further Statistics 1 only.

issues: Exact fit to chi-squared goodness-of-fit and independence nodes, but 9FM0/04B Further Statistics 2 should not be listed as eligible.
Mapping captures PGF definitions and standard-distribution PGFs but is partial for broader derivations/applications.
Paper applicability includes unsupported 9FM0/04B Further Statistics 2.
pointReviews.recommendation: Refine node mapping if more granular PGF nodes exist and remove Further Statistics 2 from eligibility.

issues: Exact node fit for mean/variance from PGFs, but 9FM0/04B Further Statistics 2 is incorrectly listed as eligible.
Exact node fit for PGF of sums of independent variables, but 9FM0/04B Further Statistics 2 is incorrectly listed as eligible.
Partial mapping: the size/power function and power-function aspects are not represented by the selected nodes.
Paper applicability includes unsupported 9FM0/04B Further Statistics 2.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a power-function or test-quality node if available, and remove Further Statistics 2 from eligibility.

issues: Partial mapping: the cumulative distribution function aspect is not covered by the selected canonical nodes.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a CDF node for continuous random variables if available, or document the partial coverage.

issues: Partial mapping: only the PDF node is used; the CDF relationship is not represented by a dedicated node.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add or map to a CDF node if one exists.

issues: Only the mean-of-continuous-RV node is selected, while the specification also requires mode, median, percentiles, skewness and E(g(X)).
pointReviews.recommendation: Create or request canonical nodes for median, mode, percentiles and skewness of continuous random variables.

issues: No canonical node exists for the continuous uniform (rectangular) distribution.
Relation is marked exact but no node is assigned, which is inconsistent.
pointReviews.recommendation: Create or request a canonical node for the continuous uniform distribution and set relation to unmapped until a node is assigned.

issues: Partial mapping: the selected correlation-coefficient nodes do not explicitly cover hypothesis testing that a correlation is zero.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a hypothesis-test node for correlation if available, or document the partial mapping.

issues: Mapping to the general Normal-model node is narrower than the specification point, which concerns linear combinations of independent Normal random variables.
pointReviews.recommendation: Create or request a canonical node for linear combinations of Normal random variables.

issues: Partial mapping: standard error, bias and quality/comparison of estimators are not fully represented by the selected nodes.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add or request nodes for standard error, bias and estimator quality if available.

issues: Mapping to the broad Estimation node is narrower than the specific confidence-interval concept in the specification.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add or request a canonical node for confidence intervals.

issues: No canonical node exists for a Normal mean confidence interval with known variance (z-interval).
Relation is marked exact but no node is assigned, which is inconsistent.
pointReviews.recommendation: Create or request a canonical node for Normal mean z-confidence intervals and set relation to unmapped until assigned.

issues: Mapping to the general mean-hypothesis-test node is narrower than the two-sample Normal means test with known variances.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add or request a canonical node for two-sample z-tests for means.

issues: Mapping to the Central Limit Theorem node is narrower than the specification point, which concerns large-sample use of sample variances.
pointReviews.recommendation: Document the narrower mapping or create a more specific node for large-sample inference with estimated variances.

issues: Partial mapping: the direct-impact-of-spheres aspect is not explicitly represented by the selected nodes.
Paper applicability incorrectly lists 9FM0/04C Further Mechanics 2 as eligible.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add the direct-impact node if available, and remove Further Mechanics 2 from eligibility.

issues: Partial mapping: vector impulse-momentum is acceptably covered but no dedicated vector-impulse node is used.
Paper applicability incorrectly lists 9FM0/04C Further Mechanics 2 as eligible.
pointReviews.recommendation: Refine node mapping if a vector-impulse node exists, and restrict eligibility to Further Mechanics 1.

issues: Source reference matches official Further Mechanics 1 section 2.1.
Canonical-node selection covers work, energy conservation and power but omits dedicated kinetic/potential-energy nodes.
Paper applicability incorrectly lists 9FM0/04C Further Mechanics 2 as eligible; FM1 content is examined in 9FM0/03C (Paper 3 Option 3C / Paper 4 Option 4C titled Further Mechanics 1), not in FM2.
pointReviews.recommendation: Remove 9FM0/04C Further Mechanics 2 from eligible papers; retain only 9FM0/03C and clarify the Paper 4 option title.

issues: Relation is marked exact, but the single centre-of-mass node does not fully cover uniform/non-uniform rigid bodies and composite bodies in official section 3.1; relation should be partial.

overallIssues.issue: Canonical node catalog lacks nodes for mathematical induction, divisibility proofs, solving linear systems by inverse matrices, and the geometric interpretation of three planes. This weakens the mappings for CP-1.1, CP-3.7 and CP-3.8 even though the source content and paper allocations are supported.

The candidate declaredPapers list contains unofficial or inconsistent optional paper labels and codes that are not present in the specification, but these are not used in the per-point paper allocations supplied in this chunk.
FP1 1.1 (t-formulae) is mapped to TRIG-IDEN-IDEN-TANR, a basic tan identity node that does not represent half-angle substitution; the semantic fit is weak.
The syllabusPointId OFFICIAL-Edexcel-9FM0-CP-5.7 uses a 'CP' (Core Pure) prefix, but section 5.7 (Combinatorics) is part of Paper 4A Further Pure Mathematics 2 (Number theory), not Core Pure.

Several candidate points are unmapped because the supplied canonical catalog has no corresponding nodes: group theory (FP2-1.1 to FP2-1.5) and L'Hospital's rule (FP1-2.4). The candidate correctly identified the unmapped status.
Tangents/normals to conics (FP1-4.3) and conic loci (FP1-4.4) map only to the parent GEOM-CONI-CONI node because the catalog lacks dedicated child nodes.

Several candidate points are mapped to overly broad, partial or no canonical nodes (e.g. continuous uniform distribution, Normal mean z-confidence interval, geometric hypothesis test, linear combinations of Normal variables, extended continuous random variable properties, and power/size of tests). The mapping should be refined or new canonical nodes created.
Two unmapped points declare relation 'exact' while supplying no canonical node. Relation should be 'unmapped' when no node is assigned.
All Further Mechanics 1 points incorrectly list 9FM0/04C Further Mechanics 2 as an eligible paper. Official Paper 4C content is Further Mechanics 2 and does not examine FM1 topics.

Decision Mathematics 1 paper allocation uses code P4D, which is ambiguous: Appendix 9 lists 4D as Decision Mathematics 2, while the specification content note states Paper 3D is also Paper 4 option 4D titled Decision Mathematics 1. The intended paper title should be explicit.

Several DM1 topics are mapped to overly generic canonical nodes that do not capture specific official requirements (bin packing, Eulerian/semi-Eulerian classification, planarity algorithm, Hamiltonian cycles).
FM2-3.1 claims an exact node relation, but the selected centre-of-mass node only partially covers uniform/non-uniform rigid bodies and composite bodies.

Several Decision Mathematics 2 topics are not represented in the canonical node catalog (transportation problems, assignment/allocation problems, dynamic programming, recurrence relations, decision analysis/EMV). The candidate correctly flags these as unmapped, but relation values should not be 'partial' when no canonical node is assigned.
DM1-4.6 maps to the generic resource-levelling node, but the specification point is a narrower scheduling technique (least-number-of-workers scheduling).

Several Appendix 1 formula points are tagged with assessmentDepth 'application', whereas the official text presents Appendix 1 as formulae students are expected to know. 'knowledge' would be the more consistent depth for these items.

Spurious candidate ID OFFICIAL-Edexcel-9FM0-CP-5.7: Core Pure Mathematics has no section 5.7. The source reference and content actually belong to Further Pure Mathematics 2 section 5.7, which is otherwise missing from the index.
The overarching themes OT1.1–OT3.5 and the assessment-objective strands are official specification requirements but are not represented in the candidate content-point index.

codexCorrections.reason: Completeness review identified omitted FP2 pages 27-29 and FS1/FM1 allocations that incorrectly included the second option.
The formula list repeats supporting formulae and is not a separate set of assessable syllabus points.
